title: Drymophloeus rumphii

It is a genus of palms native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The classification of Drymophloeus rumphii is further categorized into the subfamily Arecoideae and tribe Areceae. intro: Drymophloeus rumphii, also known as the Betel nut palm, is a species of palm tree native to the tropical forests of the Pacific Islands, particularly in Fiji, Tonga, and Samoa. The health benefits of D. rumphii include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and skin conditions.
